sections.paragraph:
- Original: σεαυτοῦσεαυτῷσεαυτόνσαυτοῦσαυτῷσαυτόν

- Transliteration: Seautou

- Phonetic: seh-ow-too'

- Definition:

1. thyself, thee

- Origin: genitive case from G4571 and G846

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: the genitive case from G4571 and G846 with the dative and accusative of the same with contractions respectively of (with to) thyself: - thee thine own self (thou) thy (-self).
